title: Figurative Language — Advanced
description: Metonymy, synecdoche, chiasmus, hyperbole, litotes — the tools of memorable prose.
summary_pt_br: Recursos avançados: metonímia (associação), sinédoque (parte pelo todo), quiasmo (ABBA), hipérbole, litotes.

scenes.subtitle_en: Metonymy, synecdoche, chiasmus, hyperbole, litotes — the tools of memorable prose.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Recursos avançados: metonímia (associação), sinédoque (parte pelo todo), quiasmo (ABBA), hipérbole, litotes.

scenes.text_en: Metonymy, synecdoche, chiasmus, hyperbole, litotes — the tools of memorable prose. Focus: figurative language — advanced. The Crown denied all involvement in the matter.
scenes.text_pt: Recursos avançados: metonímia (associação), sinédoque (parte pelo todo), quiasmo (ABBA), hipérbole, litotes. Foco: figurative language — advanced. A Coroa negou envolvimento.

scenes.title: Figurative Language — Advanced
scenes.body_en: METONYMY = substitute by association (Crown = king). SYNECDOCHE = part for whole (wheels = car). CHIASMUS = ABBA reversal (ask not what... what you). HYPERBOLE = exaggeration. LITOTES = understatement.
scenes.body_pt: Recursos avançados: metonímia (associação), sinédoque (parte pelo todo), quiasmo (ABBA), hipérbole, litotes.

examples.en: The Crown denied all involvement in the matter.
Ask not what your country can do for you, but what you can do for your country.
examples.pt: Não pergunte... mas o que você pode fazer.

examples.en: He's no genius, which means he's rather clever actually.
examples.pt: Ele não é gênio — ou seja, é bem esperto (litotes).

exercise.question: Complete: "The Crown denied all ___ in the matter."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The Crown denied all involvement in the matter."

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Ask not what your country can do for you, but what you can do for your country.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Ask not what your country can do for you, but what you can do for your country."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Não pergunte... mas o que você pode fazer.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Figurative Language — Advanced — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre figurative language — advanced. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Give one metonymy in a sentence.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Ask not what knowledge offers — offer what knowledge asks.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I could eat a horse right now.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Not a bad performance at all.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Figurative language separates memorable from forgettable.

scenes.title: The rhetoric workshop
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Identify the figurative devices used. Include at least three terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: At the rhetoric workshop, Zara projected four famous sentences and asked the class to name the device.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: The Crown denied involvement — the word 'crown' substitutes for the monarchy through metonymy.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: And 'twenty hands worked the field' uses synecdoche, letting one body part represent entire workers.

paragraphs.speaker: Zara
paragraphs.en: Chiasmus is that elegant reversal — ask not what your country can do for you, but what you can do.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Understatement, or litotes, can actually feel stronger than the direct claim — 'not bad' often means excellent.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Rhetorical devices, Zara reminded the class, are the invisible scaffolding beneath every sentence you'll ever remember.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Figurative Language — Advanced". Metonymy, synecdoche, chiasmus, hyperbole, litotes — the tools of memorable prose. The Crown denied all involvement in the matter. Ask not what your country can do for you, but what you can do for your country. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Figurative Language — Advanced". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Figurative Language — Advanced".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Figurative Language — Advanced".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
